Facts.text: Possible date of the Antioch earthquake in the Byzantine Empire (present-day Syria) which killed 200,000 people
Pope Leo III, aided by Charles the Great, returns to Rome
Black Death Massacre: Jews of Free Imperial City of Augsburg massacred amidst accusations of them causing the Bubonic Plague; part of a wave of pogroms across Western Europe
Treaty of Freiburg: French/Swiss "eternal" peace treaty
Lofland subjects himself on Sigismund August II of Poland
Don Luis de Requesensy Zuniga succeeds duke of Alva as land guardian of Netherlands
Doornik (Tournai) surrenders to Duke of Parma
Netherlands signs on to Treaty of Seville, restoring France and England's trading privileges with Spain
French commandant Beletre surrenders Detroit to Major R. Rogers
American physician James Jay invents invisible ink
Spanish expeditionary soldier José Joaquín Moraga establishes El Pueblo de San José de Guadalupe (The Town of Saint Joseph of Guadalupe) in California
Crew of slave ship Zong murders approximately 142 African slaves by throwing them into the sea to claim insurance
Chatham Islands sighted by HMS Chatham commanded by William Broughton
Dessalines and Christophe declare Saint-Domingue (Haiti) independent
November Uprising: An armed rebellion against Russia's rule beings in Poland begins
The Sonderbund is defeated by the joint forces of other Swiss cantons under General Guillaume-Henri Dufour
Cayuse Indians kill missionaries Marcus & Narcissa Whitman, and 12 others at Waiilatpu Mission in Oregon Country (near present day Walla Walla, Washington)
The treaty, Punctation of Olmütz, signed in Olomouc means diplomatic capitulation of Prussia to Austrian Empire, taking over the leadership of German Confederation
Battle of Fort Sanders, Tennessee: Confederate troops fail to break through Union defenses with 8-900 causalities
4th & last day of skirmishes at Waynesboro, Georgia
Battle of Spring Hill, Tennessee (Thomason's Station), Confederate attack on Union forces fails
Sand Creek Massacre, Colorado militia kills about 150 peaceful Cheyenne and Arapaho Indians including Cheyenne chief One-Eye
Compulsory education proclaimed in England
Indian Wars: The Modoc War begins with the Battle of Lost River
US receives rights to Pearl Harbor, on Oahu, Hawaii
1st US Army - US Navy football game, Score: Navy 24, Army 0 at West Point
Ziqiang Institute (Wuhan University) is founded by Zhang Zhidong, governor of Hubei and Hunan provinces in China's Qing Dynasty
1st motorcycle race in Surrey, England
East 182nd Street in the Bronx is paved & opened
The first US patent for inventing the traffic lights system is issued to Ernest Sirrine
Fire destroys most of the buildings on Santa Catalina Island, California
US declares martial law in Dominican Republic
A Supreme Allied War Council meets at Versailles to define war aims
Serbia annexes Montenegro
Coldest day in November in Netherlands -14.0°C
The Dawes Commission, chaired by the American Banker Charles G. Dawes, is set up to look into the German economic situation and make recommendations that the US can accept
NHL's Montreal Forum opens
W. Somerset Maughams comedic play "The Constant Wife" premieres in NYC
France & USSR sign non-aggression pact
First state liquor stores authorized in Pennsylvania
Japan begins persecution of communists
Chicago Bears beat Detroit (19-16) in 1st NFL game broadcast nationally
Dutch Prince Bernhard injured in auto accident in Netherlands
Mayor Oud of Rotterdam forbids soccer match between Netherlands and Germany
Cor Klint swims world record 200 m backstroke (2:38.8)
USSR drops diplomatic relations with Finland
Passenger ship Lurline sends radio signal of sighting Japanese war fleet
US Office of Price Administration rations coffee for everyone, 10 pound a year
U-86 sinks in Atlantic Ocean
US aircraft carrier Hornet launched
Albania liberated from Nazi control (National Day)
Johns Hopkins hospital in Baltimore performs 1st open heart surgery
The first surgery (on a human) to correct blue baby syndrome is performed by Alfred Blalock and Vivien Thomas
Yugoslavian Socialist Republic proclaimed
Dutch Minister of Social Affairs Willem Drees begins emergency rule of old age facilities
Anti-Jewish riots in Aleppo erupt after UN votes to partition Palestine, with cost of 75 lives and the disappearance of medieval manuscript the Aleppo Codex
UN General Assembly vote to allow the partition of Palestine between Arabs & Jews
1st opera to be televised, "Othello", broadcast from the Metropolitain Opera House in New York City
KOB TV channel 4 in Albuquerque, NM (NBC) begins broadcasting
Puppet TV show "Kukla, Fran, & Ollie" starring Fran Allison debuts on NBC's WNBQ in Chicago, Illinois
Nationalist regime of China leaves for Taiwan/Formosa
Uranium mine explosions in East Germany kills 3,700
National Council of Church of Christ in US forms
First underground atomic explosion at Frenchman Flat in Nevada
American Airlines begins 1st regular commercial NY-LA air service
WSIX TV channel 8 in Nashville, TN (ABC) begins broadcasting
Turkish government of Menderes resigns
English athlete Chris Brasher is disqualified for interference after finishing first in the 3,000m steeplechase at the 1956 Melbourne Olympics; decision reversed on appeal
NYC Mayor Robert Wagner forms a committee to replace baseball teams Dodgers and Giants after they relocate to the west coast
2nd Grammy Awards: "Mack The Knife", Bobby Darin win
26th Heisman Trophy Award: Joe Bellino, Navy (HB)
Freedom Riders attacked by white mob at bus station in Miss
John A McCone replaces Allen W Dulles as 6th director of CIA
Mercury-Atlas 5 carries a chimp (Enos) to orbit
Great Britain & France decide to jointly build the Concorde supersonic airliner
Major League Baseball decides to revert to 1 All-Star game per year, after 4 seasons in which 2 All-Star games were played
"I Want to Hold Your Hand" single released by the Beatles in the United Kingdom
Trans-Canada Airlines Flight 831: A Douglas DC-8 carrying 118, crashes after taking-off from Dorval Airport near Montreal, Canada
Roman Catholic Church in US replaces Latin with English
Dale Cummings does 14,118 consecutive sit-ups
1st NBA game at Oakland Coliseum Arena - Warriors beat Bulls 108-101
SS Daniel J Morrell sinks in a storm on Lake Huron, 28 die, 1 survivor.
British troops withdraw from Aden and South Yemen
The Beatles' single "Something" / "Come Together" reaches #1
1st pro golf championship at Walt Disney World
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Kilauea Volcano erupts in Hawaii
UN observes "international day of solidarity with Palestinian people," boycotted by US & about 20 other countries
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Major Iranian offensive mounted on central front
USSR performs underground nuclear test
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
A Korean Air Boeing 707 explodes over the Thai-Burmese border, killing 115
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
New Orleans Saints win, assuring their 1st winning NFL season
Ranger's Bob Frosse becomes 2nd goalie to score a goal (vs NY Islanders). It is later ruled that he should not be credited with goal.
MLB Montreal Expos president Claude Brochu agrees to buy club from Charles Bronfman
UN Security Council approves US-sponsored resolution authorizing the use of force in the Persian Gulf if Iraq does not withdrawal from Kuwait by Jan. 15, 1991
Dust storm in California's San Joaquin Valley, results in massive vehicle accident on Interstate I-5 involving 93 cars and 11 truck. near Coalinga; 17 die and 150 injured
Test Cricket debut of Javagal Srinath, v Australia at the Gabba
TV show "Roc" has a gay wedding episode - Can't Help Loving that Man
Seoul, Korea, celebrated the 600th anniversary of its founding
"Garden District" closes at Circle in the Square Theater, NYC
CNNfn, a financial network by Turner Enterprises is launched
OPEC agrees to an increase in its production ceiling. OPEC has raised the ceiling to 27.5 million barrels per day for the first half of 1998
USAir Arena closes, hosting Wash Wizards
In a classic all-Spanish final, Àlex Corretja puts on a stunning comeback to beat Carlos Moyá 3–6, 3–6, 7–5, 6–3, 7–5 and win his only ATP Tour World Championship tennis title in Hanover, Germany
UN Security Council unanimously approves a resolution extending the Oil-for-Food program in Iraq for another six-month period
The new Croatian Communist Party (KPH) is founded in Vukovar.
A 7.4 magnitude earthquake occurs off the northern coast of Martinique, affecting the Eastern Caribbean as far north as Puerto Rico and as far south as Trinidad
Armed Forces of the Philippines lay siege to The Peninsula Manila hotel after soldiers led by Senator Antonio Trillanes stage a mutiny
30 people are killed and 100 are wounded by bombs in Hillah and Karbala, Iraq
Luiz Felipe Scolari takes over as Brazilian Football coach
The UN votes to approve Palestine’s status change from an observer to an observer state
Former NFL player Darren Sharper sentenced to 20 years in prison for a series of rapes
American TV host Matt Lauer is fired from NBC's "Today" show after an allegation of sexual misconduct
Bosnian war criminal Slobodan Praljak commits suicide by poison in court at The Hague after 20 year prison term read out
Opera "Prism" composed by Ellen Reid with lyrics by Roxie Perkin premieres at REDCAT in Los Angeles (2019 Pulitzer Prize for Music)
Tens of thousands Indian farmers protest the agrarian crisis at parliament in Delhi
K-pop stars Jung Joon-young and Choi Jong-hoon sentenced to prison for gang-raping unconscious fans and distributing footage of it (Jung)
Terrorist knife attack at Fishmongers Hall by London Bridge, kills two and injures three, attacker who was previously imprisoned for 2012 terror offence is shot dead
British socialite and former girlfriend of Jeffery Epstein Ghislaine Maxwell found guilty of sex trafficking in a federal court in Manhattan
For the first time fewer than half of people in England and Wales call themselves Christian according to the 2021 Census
Malawi begins historic campaign against malaria by vaccinating children under five using the RTS,S vaccine (thought to provide 30% protection)
Singapore's parliament repeals British-era Section 377A decriminalising gay sex, but introduces amendments to protect current definition of marriage
Three stowaways rescued by Spanish coastguard after 11 days at sea balancing on a ships' rudder from Nigeria to the Canary islands
Footage of rare giant rat (Uromys vika), one of world's largest rodents, the size of a newborn baby, captured for the first time on island of Vangunu, Solomon Islands
Nepal registers its first same-sex marriage in its western Lumjung district, after becoming the second Asian country to legalize it five months earlier
US says it has foiled assassination attempt by an Indian national on a US citizen who advocates for a Sikh separatist state
KingPhilip IIdevalues Spanish currency
Bonnie Prince Charlie's Jacobite army moves into Manchester and occupies Carlisle
Napoleon's Grand Army crosses the Berezina River in retreat from Russia
Gioachino Rossini's comic opera "Il barbiere di Siviglia" (The Barber of Seville) become 1st opera performed in Italian in US, staged at New York City's Park Theater
GeneralHoratio Kitchenerassumes command of the British forces in South Africa from General Lord Roberts
Gerhart Hauptmann's play "Der arme Heinrich" premieres in Vienna
Tris Speakerresigns as manager of the Cleveland Indians while facing game-fixing allegations
Russian-French player Alexander Alekhine becomes the fourth world chess champion, defeating reigning champion CubanJosé Raúl Capablancain Buenos Aires
American aviatorRichard E. Byrdsends "My calculations indicate that we have reached vicinity of South Pole"
Michael Joseph Savagebecomes the first Labour party Prime Minister of New Zealand
PhysicistErwin Schrödingerpublishes his famous thought experiment "Schrödinger's cat", a paradox that illustrates the problem of the Copenhagen interpretation of quantum mechanics
PartisanTitoforms temporary government in Jajce, Bosnia
"The Lost Weekend," based on Charles R. Jackson's novel, directed byBilly Wilderand starringRay MillandandJane Wyman, premieres in Los Angeles (Academy Awards Best Picture 1946)
US President-electEisenhowervisits Korea to assess war
Following the failure of the Bay of Pigs invasion CIA DirectorAllen Dullesresigns and is replaced by John McCone
US PresidentLBJsets up the Warren Commission to investigate the assassination of US PresidentJohn F. Kennedy
Robert McNamaraelected president of the World Bank
John LennonandYoko Onorelease their 1st album "Two Virgins" in UK

England cricket batsmanColin Cowdreyreaches 22 on Day 3 of the drawn 1st Test against Australia in Brisbane to become Test Cricket's leading run scorer; passes countryman Wally Hammond's world Test-record aggregate of 7,249 runs
Modernist composerCharles Ives' "Yale-Princeton Football Game", a two-minute piece written in 1897 premieres
Coco the Clown's [Nicolai Poliakoff] special memorial service held at St. Paul's Cathedral, London, England
New Zealand general election won by the National Party headed byRobert Muldoon
US PresidentGerald Fordsigns Education for All Handicapped Children Act requiring states provide free education for children with a disability
NY Yankees sign free agentReggie Jacksonto 5-year contract
Joe Montanaof the SF 49ers completes an NFL record of 22 consecutive passes
Rajiv Gandhiresigns as Prime Minister of India after losing national elections
Mary J. Bligereleases "My Life," her second studio album (Billboard Music Award Top R&B Album 1995)
US PresidentBill Clintonlifts ban on exports of oil from the Alaskan North Slope; the ban was imposed after the oil embargo by Arab oil producers in 1973

"The Concert for George", a benefit memorial toGeorge Harrisonheld at the Royal Albert Hall, London, featuresEric Clapton;Paul McCartney;Ringo Starr; Jeff Lynne;Tom Petty; Joe Brown; Anoushka Shankar;Billy Preston; and members ofMonty Python
The first ofNelson Mandela's 46664 AIDS benefit concerts held at Green Point Stadium, Capetown
Nikolay Davydenko of Russia wins his only ATP World Tour Finals tennis title with a 6-3, 6-4 victory over Argentine starJuan Martín del Potroin the final in London, England
"Rolling in the Deep" single is released byAdele(Billboard Song of the Year 2011, Grammy Award for Record of the Year and Song of the Year 2012)

British Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonfinishes 2nd in season-ending Abu Dhabi F1 Grand Prix to win his 3rd overall, and 2nd consecutive World Drivers Championship by 59 points from teammate Nico Rosberg
Wood fragment believed to be from Jesus' manger returned to Bethlehem after 1400 years byPope Francis
Joe BidenandKamala Harrisannounce the first all-female Communications team for the White House
FIFA Ballon d'Or: Argentina and PSG forwardLionel Messiwins his record 7th award from Robert Lewandowski; FC Barcelona attacking midfielder Alexia Putellas is Women's World Player of the Year
Jack Dorseyannounces he is stepping down as CEO of Twitter, to be replaced by Parag Agrawal
Australian Prime MinisterAnthony Albanesemakes national apology to people effected by the thalidomide morning sickness drug over 60 years ago
Taylor Swiftis Spotify's most-streamed global artist of the year with 26.1 billion streams, andMiley Cyrus’ “Flowers” is the most-streamed song
US inventorThomas Edisondemonstrates his hand-cranked phonograph for the first time
Co-founder of Atari,Nolan Bushnell, releases Pong, the first commercially successful video game, at Andy Capp's Tavern in Sunnyvale, California
Kazan, 65th Emperor of Japan (984-6), born in Heian Kyō, Japan (d. 1008)
Lionel of Antwerp, 1st Duke of Clarence, son of Edward III of England, born in Antwerp, Buchy of Brabant (d. 1368)
Zhu Qizhen, Chinese Emperor Yingzong of Ming (1435-49 and 1457-64), born in Beijing, Ming China (d. 1464)

Gottfried Semper, German architect (Semper Opera house), born in Altona, Duchy of Holstein, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1879)

Morrison Waite, American jurist (Seventh Chief Justice of the US Supreme Court), born in Lyme, Connecticut (d. 1888)

Ludwig Anzengruber, Austrian playwright (Der Schandfleck), born in Vienna (d. 1889)

Taisen Deshimaru, Japanese founder of several Zen centers in France, born in Saga City, Japan (d. 1982)
